Play Duration: 7 minutes 16 seconds7m Brought to you by ABC Radio National Presented by Natasha Mitchell Kaye Edwards has just turned seventy. She’s just got her first tattoo and she recently reopened a rundown old tavern in the tiny rural community of Dumbleyung in the Great Southern of Western Australia. And she’s turned it into a grand country pub once more, complete with fluffy towels and a friendly resident ghost. Kaye run pubs all over Western Australia – from Borden to Pingrup and Kulin and now the Grand Olde Dumbleyung Inn. And.

Play Duration: 14 minutes 58 seconds14m When the Theatre Royal was nearly gutted by fire in 1984 many credited Fred – the resident ghost, with saving one of the Nation’s most significant and beautiful historic buildings. Now - some 34 years after the blaze – as the theatre enters a period of refurbishment – it seems timely to reflect on Australia’s oldest working theatre and celebrate its rich past. Gae Williams is with the Friends of the Theatre Royal and recently spoke with Paul McIntyre, who began by asking her about the most memorable.
title: Celebrating the history of the Theatre Royal

evidence_summary: Dig to unearth Tas convict ghost tales - ABC News Skip to main content Skip to news navigation, settings and search Explore the ABC ABC News News Home Dig to unearth Tas convict ghost tales ShareShare article Dig to unearth Tas convict ghost tales Topic:Archaeology Fri 2 Jan 2004 at 8:41amFri 2 Jan 2004 at 8:41am Fri 2 Jan 2004 at 8:41am abc.net.au/news/dig-to-unearth-tas-convict-ghost-tales/114104 Link copied ShareShare article Archaeologists are planning a dig at one of Tasmania's oldest and most mysterious burial grounds. The Isle of the Dead at Port Arthur, the focus of many myths and ghost stories, will be unearthed early this year. About 1,200 convicts and free settlers are believed to have been buried on the island during the 19th century. Archaeologist Greg Jackman plans to dispel or verify some of these myths when the dig begins next month. "The island is a place where myths.
title: Dig to unearth Tas convict ghost tales
